violations.text: Soda drain line was observed draining into the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. New Violation. Correct By: 06/30/2025
Refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair. PIC stated new gaskets have been ordered. New Violation. Correct By: 06/30/2025
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. Inspector provided RIDOH guidance. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/02/2025
The indicated handwashing sink at the bar did not have running water at the time of this inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 06/16/2025
The hand-washing sink at the bar was blocked by the POS system. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. New Violation. Correct By: 06/16/2025

Molluscan shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. New Violation. Correct By: 06/02/2025

Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination. Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented. New Violation. Correct By: 06/02/2025

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the bar dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. PIC called for service during inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 06/16/2025
Chili and soup were observed with date marks exceeding 7 days. PIC disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/02/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of staff, soup and sauce were placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. PIC placed soup and sauce in shallow containers. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/02/2025

Working containers of sauces/dressings are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 06/16/2025

Buckets of various foods were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. PIC began to relocated buckets during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/02/2025
Shelving in the dry storage area is not 6 inches above the ground. New Violation. Correct By: 06/16/2025

Bowls are being used as scoops in containers of tuna salad. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. New Violation. Correct By: 06/16/2025
The ice scoop was stored with the handle in direct contact with ice in the ice machine. Inspector placed ice scoop on hook during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/02/2025

The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the dish machines. Inspector provided during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/02/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: -refrigeration gaskets -bottom shelving of prep units -under grill -slicer -can opener base. New Violation. Correct By: 06/16/2025
The outside dumpster is not covered. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered. New Violation. Correct By: 06/02/2025
No person was certified in food safety at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 06/18/2024
Oil and onions were observed stored on the floor in the dry storage area. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 06/04/2024
No person was certified in food safety at the time of inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/21/2024

Per interview of Chef, sauce is left out during service. Sauce was observed held at 89 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/21/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Lentil soup was placed hot in a covered five-gallon pail, which was observed cooling at 114-135 degrees Fahrenheit in the walk-in cooler. Foods are to be cooled in shallow pans no greater than 4" deep to allow for rapid cooling. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/21/2024
Soup was observed stored on the floor in the walk-in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. PIC informed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/21/2024
Sauce was observed stored on the ground outside. Food and drinks must be stored in the establishment. PIC informed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/21/2024
Prep sink was observed with an inadequate air gap. PIC informed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/21/2024
No person in charge was certified in food safety at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 05/21/2024
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was blocked by a tray. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/07/2024

Shellstock tags are not maintained for 90 days. Shellstock tags or labels are required to be maintained for 90 calendar days from the date the container is emptied. New Violation. Correct By: 05/07/2024

Raw animal foods (eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (drinks) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/07/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of black/brown residue. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 05/07/2024

Chicken stock was observed held at 72 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/07/2024

Various prepared foods were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/07/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of sous chef, soup was placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. PIC informed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/07/2024
Various foods were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 05/07/2024
Onions were observed stored on the floor outside. Food and drinks must be stored in establishment. PIC informed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/07/2024
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the dishwashers. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 05/14/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the dish racks have an accumulation of black/brown residue. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 05/21/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the hoods have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 05/21/2024
Prep sink was observed with an inadequate air gap. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 05/21/2024
